raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "moral comparison and ranking of passions",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "anger constitutes a grief-driven withdrawal from reason",
    "lust constitutes surrender to pleasure revealing character weakness",
    "a 'manlike' disposition is defined by resistance to pleasure-based impulses",
    "sins can be hierarchically evaluated by their relation to reason and potency"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent within a Stoic framework that equates virtue with rational self-mastery and treats passions as diagnostic of character",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "acceptance of reason as the sovereign human faculty and agreement that classifying passions by their effect on agency aids ethical self-regulation",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "rejection of the reason-passion binary, denial that gendered ideals of 'manliness' track moral worth, or empirical evidence that anger and lust are not reliably distinguishable in motivational structure"
}
